summary: Kill workload executor when astrolabe exits with an error
description:
Currently when astrolabe exits with an error it does not kill the workload executor, if one is running. This causes two types of issues:

- On a local machine, the workload executor continues running in the background, potentially spamming terminal with warnings/errors if it cannot connect to cluster or perform an operation
- In evergreen, the test run gets timed out because evergreen uses go and go waits for all processes in a spawned process tree to exit, not just the spawned process itself (https://github.com/golang/go/issues/20730)

{code}
[2021/10/08 17:22:12.455] W, [2021-10-08T17:22:12.200850 #22238]  WARN -- : MONGODB | Error running awaited hello on 2e694dd263-shard-00-02.80104.mongodb-qa.net:27017: Mongo::Error::SocketError: Errno::ECONNREFUSED: Connection refused - connect(2) for 54.176.135.219:27017 (for 54.176.135.219:27017 (2e694dd263-shard-00-02.80104.mongodb-qa.net:27017, TLS)) (on 2e694dd263-shard-00-02.80104.mongodb-qa.net:27017)

[2021/10/08 19:03:26.166] Running task-timeout commands.
{code}

To avoid these issues astrolabe should attempt to terminate the workload executor if it launched one and astrolabe is exiting with an error.

summary: Raise client error for snapshot sessions on <5.0 servers
Disable writes on snapshot sessions
description:
The snapshot reads spec has been updated to require drivers send readConcern with all commands in a snapshot reads session, even writes. This way we rely on the server to report an error for unsupported snapshot read operations.

See the spec change: https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/8ade331b8a8be3e5c3a35b224aa16d5d6927ebf0

And the new spec section:
https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/sessions/snapshot-sessions.rst#sending-readconcern-to-the-server-on-all-commands

h3. Original description:

Writes on snapshot reads sessions should be disabled, to eliminate possible users mistakes.

summary: Unpin sessions after all abortTransaction attempts
description:
*New Description*

To address the issue described in the original description, we've decided that all abortTransaction attempts, whether successful or unsuccessful, should unpin the ClientSession. If the abort attempt fails with a retryable error, we have to unpin before doing the retry anyway. If it fails with a non-retryable error, unpinning is still useful because the user cannot call abortTransaction again per the transactions FSM, so the transaction is still considered complete.

It may be convenient to address this issue concurrently with DRIVERS-1638 and DRIVERS-1685.

*Previous Description*

Consider the following scenario
 # abortTransaction attempt 1
 # Server responds with {{ShutdownInProgress}} without TransientTransactionError labels
 # Server stays pinned and in Unknown state (it's unpinned only on TransientTransactionError/UnknownTransactionCommitResult labels)
 # abortTransaction attempt 2 fails due to server Unknown state

Need to determine whether ShutdownInProgress should include TransientTransactionError for abortTransaction or should it be decorated on driver side.
 Tests affected: [retryable-abort|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/transactions/tests/retryable-abort.json]

summary: Don't check for 'dropped' or 'info' fields in response of dropDatabase command
description:
{panel:title=Downstream Change Summary|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}
    This commit changed the response of a dropDatabase command.

In particular I've removed the following two optional fields from the response:
 - dropped
 - info

https://github.com/mongodb/mongo/commit/b134809bd5db091c0517ee0fb4378ad420a5c021#diff-9272988c09b014f8a2e329f5e295fdb3bef4b75f18a5bd89e92361c23efe1851L40-L47

These fields were not documented anywhere and were not reliable in case of stepdowns/failovers.
    {panel}

    h2. Description of Linked Ticket
    Currently when dropping a nonexistent database, we return a response that contains information about if the database previously existed or not.
  - [On replicaset|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo/blob/7398420049d667760624e6fd36237ddffe95ed4d/src/mongo/db/commands/dbcommands.cpp#L211-L217]
  - [On sharded cluster|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo/blob/7398420049d667760624e6fd36237ddffe95ed4d/src/mongo/s/commands/cluster_drop_database_cmd.cpp#L114-L118]

This response is not reliable if a step-down happens during the operation, in fact on step-down the operation is automatically retried on the new replicaset primary. On this new node we don't have any way of assessing previous existence of the database because the old primary could have either already drop it or not.

Moreover the drop database response is not currently documented anywhere.

The goal of this ticket is to simplify the drop database response by not returning any information on the previous state of the database being dropped.

summary: Add BSON Binary subtype 7
Ensure tests on new servers aren't relying on OP_KILL_CURSORS, OP_INSERT, OP_UPDATE, and OP_DELETE
description:
{panel:title=Downstream Change Summary|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}
    The server won't accept requests that use OP_DELETE, OP_INSERT, OP_UPDATE or OP_KILLCURSORS opcodes anymore and will close the connection.
    {panel}

    h2. Description of Linked Ticket
    We still need to bump the associated opcounters and log the warning as we do in 5.0.

This task should remove the code which becomes unreachable.

If necessary, we can split this into separate tasks in the future for OP_KILL_CURSORS and the write ops.

summary: Serverless create-instance script should handle wider range of project names
description:
The [create-instance.sh|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/blob/master/.evergreen/serverless/create-instance.sh] script is responsible for creating serverless instances.  It creates the instance names based on the Evergreen project name, which is set in the PROJECT environment variable.  The problem is that common project names cause Atlas to return errors.  For example, the project name "mongo-java-driver-4.4" results in an this error:

bq. The serverless instance name 697-mongo-java-driver-4.4 is invalid. The name can only contain ASCII letters, numbers, and hyphens.

while the project name "mongo-java-driver-44" results in this error:

bq. Serverless instance name \"9974-mongo-java-driver-44\" is invalid. Atlas truncates serverless instance names to 23 characters which results in an invalid hostname due to a trailing \"-\" in the generated serverless instance name prefix \"9974-mongo-java-driver-\".

Given that there are many existing projects that don't work with this script, I suggest that we modify this script so that it tolerates a wider range of project names, taking into account whatever rules Atlas has for serverless instance names.  Maybe the serverless instance name shouldn't depend on the project name at all.  Not sure what the benefit of that is.

summary: Operationalize sanity check that Astrolabe runs fail with disabled retryability
description:
In scope of DRIVERS-1922 we confirmed that Astrolabe runs fail as expected when retryability is disabled.  The next step is to operationalize that, so that in the future if that assumption changes we will be notified of it.

So for all drivers, we should run a task with retryability disabled (read and write) and have the test assertion reversed: i.e. the task should fail if there are no errors, and succeed if there are errors.

Note: it's possible that every once in a while we get "lucky" and there are no errors despite retryability being disabled.  If we see that behavior, it's not clear what the alternative is.

summary: Use OP_MSG for exhaust cursors if the server supports OP_MSG
description:
*Background & Motivation* 

Exhaust cursors with OP_QUERY currently error against 5.1 development servers with the server change in SERVER-57391.

This only applies to C and Ruby. See the spreadsheet [Drivers affected by removal of OP_QUERY (SERVER-57391 )|https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1cRICpmQbaitAFCsCAAM1XHun3Ba8qIgjjY1Iot3RNnU/edit#gid=0].

This is required in drivers that support exhaust cursors for 5.1 to avoid a loss of functionality.

*Scope*
 Update the [Find, getMore, killCursors|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/df6be82f865e9b72444488fd62ae1eb5fca18569/source/find_getmore_killcursors_commands.rst#exhaust] specification to note that drivers must use OP_MSG if the server supports OP_MSG.

Add a test for establishing an exhaust cursor. This may need to be a prose test since not all drivers support exhaust cursors.

Drivers that already support exhaust cursors with OP_QUERY must update to use OP_MSG.

Drivers that do not support exhaust cursors must not make any changes. The proposal to specify exhaust cursor behavior in DRIVERS-535 was rejected because there was no compelling use case.

*Original Description*
{panel:title=Downstream Change Summary|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}
OP_QUERY and OP_GET_MORE messages aren't supported anymore and the server would respond to them with an error OP_REPLY message.
{panel}
h2. Description of Linked Ticket

As part of removing support for OP_QUERY or OP_GET_MORE, the server should return an error indicating that these wire protocol op codes are no longer supported.

However, we need to make sure that the server continues to accept OP_QUERY isMaster commands so that the connection handshake continues to work.

We should ensure that we still count attempted uses of OP_QUERY and OP_GET_MORE in {{db.serverStatus().opcounters.deprecated}}. We must also continue to log warning id 5578800, e.g.:
{noformat}{"t":{"$date":"2021-06-03T11:35:40.983-04:00"},"s":"W",  "c":"COMMAND",  "id":5578800, "ctx":"conn1","msg":"Deprecated operation requested","attr":{"op":"query","clientInfo":{"application":{"name":"MongoDB Shell"},"driver":{"name":"MongoDB Internal Client","version":"5.0.0-alpha0"},"os":{"type":"Linux","name":"Ubuntu","architecture":"x86_64","version":"18.04"}}}}
{noformat}

summary: Auth replsets should use arbiterOnly:true like other replset configs
description:
When launching clusters using {{mongo-orchestration}}, {{auth.json}} and {{auth-ssl.json}} removed {{arbiterOnly:true}} back in 2018 to work around SERVER-34421. After SERVER-34421 was resolved, these configuration changes were never reverted. This resulted in auth-enabled replica sets in our test environments running with 3 data-bearing nodes and no arbiter.

Unfortunately 27017 and 27018 ran with a 500MB oplog, but 27019 ran with a 100MB oplog. This led to sporadic node failures if 27018 ended up tailing 27019 - which could result in 27018 falling off the oplog and fasserting as happened in the .NET/C# driver during times of heavy oplog churn.

This ticket is to revert [this commit|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/commit/a8723971bb0d9ce6bfefdf3cc42c661b856e4706] from {{drivers]evergreen-tools}} so that all replica set test configurations run with two data-bearing nodes (27017 and 27018) and one arbiter (27019).

summary: Investigate changes in SERVER-60839: Introduce a TemporarilyUnavailable error type
description:
{panel:title=Original Downstream Change Summary|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}
    SERVER-60839 introduces a new TemporarilyUnavailable error type. This error indicates that the operation aborted, likely due to excessive server load.
This error type is only returned when the server is running with the loadShedding parameter enabled. Serverless plans to enable the loadShedding parameters, and to handle TemporarilyUnavailable errors without returning the error code to the end client/driver. Non-serverless instances are not supposed to enable load shedding, because current drivers are unaware of this error code.
    {panel}
    h2. Description of Linked Ticket
    The TemporarilyUnavailable error indicates that the operation has been aborted, likely due to excessive server load (e.g. transaction rolled back for eviction). This error is retried in the server with an increasingly larger backoff. Internal operations are retried indefinitely, user operations are retried up to a fixed number of attempts before returning TemporarilyUnavailable to the client.
 
------
 
Original title: Instead of WriteConflict, return a more specialized error when oldest transactions are rolled back for eviction
Original description: Currently, when a write operation is hitting the wt dirty threshold limit, we take the error from WiredTiger, a WT_ROLLBACK, and up-convert to a WriteConflict. This is misleading and should print something more specific instead. Something that would indicate the actual reason.

summary: Update Go version in ADL build script
description:
{panel:bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

_ADL tests are failing setup because ADL now requires Go1.17+ to build. We are still using Go version 1.16 [here|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/blob/master/.evergreen/atlas_data_lake/build-mongohouse-local.sh#L15]._
h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?
Drivers devs.

h4. How does this affect the end user?
Failing test setups.

h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?
Unavoidable in test setup.

h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?
Loss of test coverage.

h4. Is this issue urgent?
No.

h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?
No.

h4. Is this ticket only for tests?
Yes.

{panel}

summary: Investigate changes in SERVER-56934: Improve collMod API for time-series expireAfterSeconds
Enable $locf without a feature flag
description:
{panel:title=Original Downstream Change Summary|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}
    This exposes the $locf window function. See the syntax document in the epic for details.
    h2. Description of Linked Ticket
    null

summary: Drivers must raise an error if response messageLength > ismaster.maxMessageSizeBytes
description:
When reading a response from the server, if header.messageLength is greater than ismaster.maxMessageSizeBytes a driver *MUST* raise an error and close the connection.

If this is the first operation run on the server (e.g. the first ismaster command sent on first connection) use a hard coded value of 48MB, switching to ismaster.maxMessageSizeBytes once you have received a valid ismaster response.

Original bug report continues below
---------------------------------------------

The new {{find()}} command in MongoDB 3.2 can return documents that are larger then the configured maxBsonObjectSize (16777216 bytes).

Drivers that validate the messageLength header from the server need to adjust their validation accordingly.

I suggest we use the same logic as the server, which is rumored to do:
maxBsonObjectSize + 16K

summary: Remove use of admin database in CSFLE driver doc examples
Split legacy batch inserts into many messages
description:
Split batch inserts into multiple messages and if safe writes stop after first error if not continueOnError or combine errors if so.

|| || fail on error || continueOnError ||
|| acknowledged | * after each batch, send the requested getlasterror.  If response is !ok, send no more batches and report an error | * after each batch, send the requested getlasterror. Save the last !ok response. 
* after the last batch, report the last !ok response |
|| unacknowledged | * after each batch but the last, send {getlasterror : 1}.  If response is !ok, send no more batches and return (no error reported) 
* after the last batch, send no getlasterror command | * after each batch, send no getlasterror command |

summary: Add "How to Ask for Help" section to Github readme
description:
[~acm] added a section to the C++ Github readme that greatly improved the quality of support requests:

https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-cxx-driver/wiki/Download%20and%20Compile#how-to-ask-for-help

The goal is to include a list of all information you'll require to work on a support issue, including examples, to help keep back-and-forths down to a minimum. 

All drivers should include their own version of this as an extension of the "Support" section of the readme.

summary: Ability to use different Service Name on the driver for Kerberos Authentication
description:
It is desirable for the drivers to support the capability to use an alternative *Service Name*. This is frequently a requirement of role segregation as mandated by regulation such as Sarbanes-Oxley.

Kerberos has the notion of a Service Principal Name, or SPN.  The SPN consists of a *Service Name* and a fully qualified domain name (FQDN).  So, an example SPN is mongodb/localhost:8920.  In this example, the FQDN is localhost:8920 and the *Service Name* is mongodb.

The need identified in this ticket is to support an alternative *Service Name*.  In the above example, for instance, it would be to change "mongodb" to "fluffy".

The Drivers Authentication spec has this detailed out here: https://wiki.10gen.com/display/10GEN/Driver+Authentication.

The two places you'll need to make changes are:
# In section 5.1 where we need a map for additional mechanism parameters.
#* in particular, the additional mechanism parameter necessary would be for the service name.
# In section 6.1 where we need a way to provide the service on the connection string.  It will take the form of "gssapiServiceName" with the value being the service name to use.

summary: Clarify expected bulk write operations for rewrapManyDataKey
description:
{panel:bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

The CSE spec does not fully describe what bulk write operations are expected given a call to {{{}ClientEncryption.rewrapManyDataKey(){}}}. The CSE spec should capture the expectations currently expressed only as [assertions in unified spec tests|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/41b335af92c12258a4cbe7e93ad4f34ef31641a7/source/client-side-encryption/tests/unified/rewrapManyDataKey.yml].
h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?

Drivers implementing {{{}ClientEncryption.rewrapManyDataKey(){}}}.
h4. How does this affect the end user?

Clarification of expected bulk write operations in CSE spec may reduce confusion or opportunity for surprise during implementation.
h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?

N/A
h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?

Minor.
h4. Is this issue urgent?

No.
h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?

No.
h4. Is this ticket only for tests?

No. This clarifies expectations of implementation of {{{}ClientEncryption.rewrapManyDataKey(){}}}.
{panel}

summary: Support Removal of Several Platform Builds
description:
Several Community and Enterprise build variants have been removed from master (5.0), we should ensure we follow suit. 

* RHEL7 ppc64le builds removed from master (5.0) SERVER-53729
* Ubuntu 18.04 ppc64le builds removed from master (5.0) SERVER-53730
* Ubuntu 18.04 s390x builds removed from master (5.0) SERVER-50568
* SLES 12 s390x build removed from master (5.0) SERVER-53728
* RHEL7 s390x builds removed from master (5.0) SERVER-53727
* RHEL 6 s390x builds removed from master (5.0) SERVER-53726

summary: Bump maxWireVersion for MongoDB 6.0
description: The maxWireVersion for MongoDB 6.0 is 17. Drivers should bump their maxWireVersion to 17 for purposes of the [version compatibility check|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/server-discovery-and-monitoring/server-discovery-and-monitoring.rst#checking-wire-protocol-compatibility].

summary: Randomize project names with random value
description:
{panel:borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#21313c|titleBGColor=#21313c|titleColor=#fafbfc|bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

Currently Astrolabe uses salt + timestamp to produce the project name. This can cause multiple tests running at the same time to get the same exact project name, which would subsequently cause Astrolabe to attempt to create the same project twice and fail.

A random value should be added to the generated project name for each test run to avoid this issue.

This manifests as the following error:

{code}
[2022/06/08 05:12:28.972] atlasclient.exceptions.AtlasApiError: 404: Not Found. Error Code: 'NOT_ATLAS_GROUP': Group 62a02fbc189fa56883c86716 is not an Atlas group; use the Cloud Manager Public API at /api/public/v1.0 to access it. (GET https://cloud-qa.mongodb.com/api/atlas/v1.0/groups/byName/drivers-atlas-testing-1654665148)
{code}

https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task_log_raw/drivers_atlas_testing_tests_dotnet_windows__driver~dotnet_master_platform~windows_64_runtime~dotnet_async_netcoreapp2.1_failure_write_62a02cd157e85a577146cbd3_22_06_08_05_00_01/0?type=T&text=true
￼

h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?
Driver developers.
h4. How does this affect the end user?
Users see test failures when there is no genuine failure to solve.
h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?
The problem occurs rather frequently because many tests are scheduled to start at the same exact time (20+ across all languages).
h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?
The problem causes spurious test failures.
h4. Is this issue urgent?
No
h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?
No
h4. Is this ticket only for tests?
No

{panel}

summary: Remove "journal" block from all mongo-orchestration server configurations
description:
{panel:borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#21313c|titleBGColor=#21313c|titleColor=#fafbfc|bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

Now that server v6.1.0-rc0 is available, the "journal" configuration block appears to no longer be supported (see SERVER-55208). Additionally, the "journal" block in the server configurations specifies the default behavior for all supported server versions, so including it is redundant. Remove the "journal" configuration block from all {{mongo-orchestration}} server configurations.

h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?
DBX devs.

h4. How does this affect the end user?
Driver CI builds that use the "latest" server version may fail consistently for reasons unrelated to the driver.

h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?
It will fail consistently for all CI tasks using the "latest" server version on platforms where the "latest" server version is v6.1.0-rc0 or greater.

h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?
Failing CI builds cause unnecessary noise and may hide real issues.

h4. Is this issue urgent?
No.

h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?
No.

h4. Is this ticket only for tests?
No.
{panel}

summary: Densification for numerical/date domains
description:
{panel:title=Downstream Change Summary|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}
    Add an aggregation stage $densify to add documents in a sequence of documents where certain values in a field are missing.
    {panel}

    h2. Description of Linked Ticket
    {panel:title=Epic Summary|borderStyle=dashed|borderColor=#cccccc|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}
h4. Summary

Provide a way to add missing documents given an order domain, e.g. adding documents for missing days in a collection ordered by time with date granularity.
h4. Motivation

This is a foundational feature in a time series database and will be expected by our customers in our MVP.
h4. Cast of Characters
 * Product Owner: Katya Kamenieva
 * Project Lead: TBD
 * Program Manager: Joe Kanaan
 * Drivers Contact: TBD

h4. Documentation

[Scope Document|http://tinyurl.com/y8ydvcjc]
 [Technical Design Document|http://tinyurl.com/y952h4xr]
[Syntax Document|https://tinyurl.com/j767aru8]
 [Product Description|http://tinyurl.com/densgapfill]
{panel}

summary: Investigate changes in SERVER-64205: Remove featureFlagLoadBalancer feature flag
description:
{panel:title=Original Downstream Change Summary|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}
    The featureFlagLoadBalancer server parameter has been removed.
    h2. Description of Linked Ticket
    null

summary: Resync SDAM tests
description: Added some mixed case hostnames into the SDAM test corpus. Drivers should sync tests to [0dc6496|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/0dc64964e91929068f47d95f7121d5312d763d77].

summary: Do not include crypt_shared on latest when less than 6.0
description:
{panel:bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

_Enterprise auth tests are failing on Ubuntu 16.04 in the Go driver because we're unable to fetch the crypt_shared package on "latest". "latest" on Ubuntu 16.04 is less than 6.0, so crypt_shared is not even available. We should not fetch crypt_shared on  OSes where "latest" is less than 6.0._

*Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?

_Driver devs._
h4. How does this affect the end user?

_Some OSes may fail to fetch crypt_shared and cause system failures._
h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?

_Always on those OSes._
h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?

_System failure of auth tests._
h4. Is this issue urgent?

_No._
h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?

_No._
h4. Is this ticket only for tests?

_Yes._
{panel}

summary: Increase documented Astrolabe minimum Python version to 3.7
description:
{panel:bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

Increase documented Astrolabe minimum Python version from 3.5 to 3.7. The main motivator is easier logging using [f-strings|https://docs.python.org/3/reference/lexical_analysis.html#f-strings].

Check out all of what's new in [3.6|https://docs.python.org/3/whatsnew/3.6.html] and [3.7|https://docs.python.org/3/whatsnew/3.7.html].

h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?
Astrolabe developers.

h4. How does this affect the end user?
They are limited to the features of Python 3.5.

h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?
Astrolabe does a lot of logging. Currently, nearly every log message needs to use explicit string format calls.

h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?
Annoyance.

h4. Is this issue urgent?
No.

h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?
No.

h4. Is this ticket only for tests?
No.
{panel}

summary: Inherited comment causes OperationFailure on MongoDB <4.4.0
description: While implementing DRIVERS-2189 for the Python Driver (PYTHON-3119) I found a bug caused by the fact that "comment" is not supported for getMore commands on MongoDB <4.4.0. The issue is that for find/agg commands "comment" has been supported at the top level since before 4.4.0, but when the driver then inherits those comments to the getMore, the user will experience an OperationFailure when using MongoDB <4.4.0.

summary: Fix comment helper specification tests
description:
{panel:bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

Currently there are a few minor issues in the specification tests for the comment field. Usage of non-$-operators for updates, and missing "$$unsetormatches" in a few spots.

h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?

Driver developers trying to implement the comment field.

h4. Is this ticket only for tests?

It is just for tests
{panel}

summary: Add Apple M1 downloads to drivers-evergreen-tools
description:
{panel:borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#21313c|titleBGColor=#21313c|titleColor=#fafbfc|bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*
On an Apple M1 Mac, {{drivers-evergreen-tools}} currently downloads the x64 tarballs, which works fine for the server, but causes {{crypt_shared_v1.dylib}} to fail as the x64 dylib cannot be loaded into an arm64 process.

h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?
DBX engineers
h4. How does this affect the end user?
Cannot test CSFLE shared library on Apple M1 Mac in an arm64 native process.
h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?
Always occurs when testing CSFLE shared library on Apple M1. You cannot load a x64 dylib into an arm64 process.
h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?
Any CSFLE shared library tests will fail.
h4. Is this issue urgent?
ASAP.
h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?
No.
h4. Is this ticket only for tests?
This only affects testing.

{panel}

summary: Catch all Astrolabe setup exceptions and mark them as setup failures instead of task failures
description:
{panel:bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*
Some exceptions that can happen during Astrolabe setup are not recognized as part of setup and are registered as task failures instead of setup failures. Update Astrolabe exception handling to catch _all_ exceptions that can happen during setup and defer handling those errors to the "check-cloud-failure" command that runs after the main test run.

h4. Detailed Description
The Atlas cluster setup happens during the [run-one command|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-atlas-testing/blob/8b3ace4521c5ca9b589412d5ecfb471113e83565/.evergreen/config.yml#L98-L115], which is configured as type "test" in the Evergreen config (displays as "test failure"). It seems like we really want to check for cloud failure in the [check-cloud-failure command|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-atlas-testing/blob/8b3ace4521c5ca9b589412d5ecfb471113e83565/.evergreen/config.yml#L116-L122], which is configured as type "setup" in the Evergreen config (displays as "setup failure"). Deferring cloud setup failure checking depends on [this try/except block|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-atlas-testing/blob/8b3ace4521c5ca9b589412d5ecfb471113e83565/astrolabe/runner.py#L475-L483] in the runner, which expects a very specific set of exception types and error messages. However, some of those HTTP timeout exceptions are thrown from HTTP calls in {{__init__}} functions (e.g. [here|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-atlas-testing/blob/8b3ace4521c5ca9b589412d5ecfb471113e83565/astrolabe/runner.py#L424-L425]) and aren't caught by the try/except block.

The [exception handling block|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-atlas-testing/blob/8b3ace4521c5ca9b589412d5ecfb471113e83565/astrolabe/runner.py#L475-L483] that attempts to defer errors caused by cloud setup to a following "setup"-type Evergreen command doesn't handle a lot of possible setup exceptions. We need to refactor the exception handling logic to catch all exceptions that can happen during initialization and cluster setup (e.g. by moving the try/except block to where the runner is initialized and called [here|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-atlas-testing/blob/8b3ace4521c5ca9b589412d5ecfb471113e83565/astrolabe/cli.py#L447-L458]).
 
h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?

People supporting Astrolabe. Possibly driver devs who are erroneously notified about drivers failures.
h4. How does this affect the end user?

An Astrolabe build will fail with a task failure instead of a setup failure.
h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?

Reasonably likely, especially if deployments to the "Cloud QA" Atlas environment are happening during an Astrolabe run.
h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?

It is confusing to someone trying to debug the Astrolabe build failure.
h4. Is this issue urgent?

No.
h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?

No.
h4. Is this ticket only for tests?

Yes.
{panel}

summary: Always report 'wallTime' in the change stream event output
description:
{panel:title=Original Downstream Change Summary|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}
    Change stream events now include a 'wallTime' field with the wall time from the mongod that the change event originated from. previously this was only exposed if the showExpandedEvents change stream option was set to true.
    h2. Description of Linked Ticket
    We currently have the 'wallTime' field guarded behind the 'showExpandedEvents' flag. This was done so that we don't add this output field directly into API version 1. Given the requests from the users, we thought it would be a good idea to always expose this.

summary: Fix getMore with comment specs
description:
{panel:bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

[DRIVERS-2189] added specs for {{getMore}} command with a comment. There are two problems with those tests:

1. {{minServerVersion}} should be 4.4 since this is the minimal version that supports comment on getMore (see [docs|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/command/getMore/]).
2. Those tests should not be run against a sharded cluster. On sharded cluster we can get empty GetMore commands (see [spec|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/tree/master/source/change-streams/tests#testing-on-sharded-clusters]).

h4. Is this issue urgent?
No. It might be easier to implement this together with [DRIVERS-742], but this is not required.


h4. Is this ticket only for tests?

Yes.
{panel}

summary: Fix Astrolabe Ruby workload executor integration
Sharing a MongoClient for metadata lookup can lead to deadlock in drivers using automatic encryption
Update Go version 1.18+ in ADL build script
description:
{panel:bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

_ADL tests are failing setup because ADL now requires Go1.18+ to build. We are still using Go version 1.17 [here|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/blob/master/.evergreen/atlas_data_lake/build-mongohouse-local.sh#L15]._
h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?

Drivers devs.
h4. How does this affect the end user?

Failing test setups.
h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?

Unavoidable in test setup.
h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?

Loss of test coverage.
h4. Is this issue urgent?

No.
h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?

No.
h4. Is this ticket only for tests?

Yes.
{panel}

summary: Fix Astrolabe missing Python 3.9 binary
Regenerate test certificates with SHA256 signatures
description:
The test certs that Hannes once copied from mongo-c-driver to drivers-evergreen-tools use an outdated signature hash algorithm. A Motor user has noticed that some Debians ban these certs:

[https://github.com/mongodb/motor/pull/43]

Let's regenerate them with SHA256 signatures and keep the script that generated them so the next regeneration task will be easier.

summary: Investigate changes in SERVER-61909: Hang inserting or deleting document with large number of index entries
Investigate changes in SERVER-64289: Platform Support: Add support for RHEL7 PPC in 5.0.X
description:
{panel:title=Original Downstream Change Summary|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}
    RHEL7-ppc builds are added back to 5.0 version
    h2. Description of Linked Ticket
    null

summary: allow limit in $filter expression
Expose $documents as external stage
Support AWS authentication with temporary credentials in CSFLE
description:
Currently AWS authentication is only available through accessKeyId and secretAccessKey. To support temporary credentials, drivers must support an additional sessionToken in the [kmsProviders configuration|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/client-side-encryption/client-side-encryption.rst#kmsproviders].

Most of the work is in libmongocrypt (MONGOCRYPT-302), which was released in 1.2.0. Drivers may need to tweak bindings to configure AWS credentials with {{mongocrypt_setopt_kms_providers}} instead of {{mongocrypt_setopt_kms_provider_aws}}.

This adds new JSON/YAML spec tests for passing temporary credentials as an AWS KMS provider. Drivers can obtain temporary credentials using [set-temp-credentials.sh in driver-evergreen-tools|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/blob/master/.evergreen/csfle/set-temp-creds.sh].

summary: Add test for security-sensitive command monitoring event redaction
description:
Drivers should sync new command-monitoring spec tests (in the {{unified/}} directory) with [mongodb/specifications@352d911|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/tree/352d9115228873271b4385d399f1e4981a9df1dd]. Test runners need to be updated to support the {{observeSensitiveCommands}} property added in Unified Test Format schema version 1.5. Drivers that have not yet implemented all features required to support test schema version 1.5 should avoid a delay in integrating these tests into their suites by either adding support for the {{observeSensitiveCommands}} property and special-casing support for schemaVersion 1.5 (drivers will need to manually ensure that they don't sync tests bearing schemaVersion 1.5 or earlier that use features not yet implemented in their test runners to avoid failures).

Older, non-unified spec tests were moved to a {{legacy/}} directory in commit ([39ccfce|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/39ccfce33adae2940ee24e7a7ad68fb3f9125b64]).

----

The command monitoring specification requires redaction of security-sensitive commands. See [https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/command-monitoring/command-monitoring.rst#security].

There was a recent regression related to this requirement that resulted in a CVE.

There are no specification tests for this requirement, either YAML or prose. Let's add some to avoid future regressions and ensure that all drivers actually implement this requirement.

summary: Log time spent waiting for remote operations
description:
{panel:title=Original Downstream Change Summary|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}
    This change adds a new optional field, "remoteOpWaitMillis" to the profiler / slow query log lines.  It tells you how much time the node spent waiting for results from other nodes.  By comparing this with the total "durationMillis" you can figure out whether the merger or a shard is to blame for a slow query.

This change basically expands 'remoteOpWaitMillis' to any command that contacts shards.
    h2. Description of Linked Ticket
    This ticket is the find() counterpart of SERVER-31368 which addresses aggregation.

summary: Clarify behavior for runOn.minServerVersion for latest servers
description: If spec tests mention the latest server like here [https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/c6645365fb68e9427f8599196a790819942f4e64#diff-4cfbf0e747c1c274f4904f3495b64c25f41c24302a3275ee562ab0af5c7dd37cR602] (without considering prerelease versions) mostlikely these tests will be skipped by test runners since the "prerelease" < "release". For example, if the current latest is `4.9.0-pre1`, the test from the above spec will be skipped because "4.9.0-pre1" < "4.9.0". I think we need to ignore prerelease versions in JSON tests.

summary: Cursor iteration should complete (abnormally) when another thread closes the cursor
